publicationStmt.p: Transcribed from Bodleian Library, Lovelace Byron collection, Box 165, folios 144-145. Transcription by Lisa Gold (2022). TEI conversion for Sextant pipeline, 2026.

div.p: Dearest William. I arrived here at six this morning; & now write from an Hotel, where I came to recruit myself & take a warm bath previous to going to the Hen. I should have been with her two hours since but that it is has been pouring with rain the whole morning. I am now hoping to go & surprise her (by the bye you know she has omitted giving me her address;-- further than that she has left the Rue de Rivole), very shortly, as it looks much brighter.-- I had a very rapid & excellent journey, tho' of course I can say nothing for its agreeableness, or for the comfort of the night I have passed. But I have got to my journey's end, & with entire success. After I wrote my letter to you on

board the Packet the other day, I became on approaching the Foreland, most distressingly ill. It was not sickness, but an effect on my circulation & nervous system-- the combined results of the sea air (which is known to be lowering in its tendency), & the motion which latter was very considerable, & our Boat in all respects small, bad, and uncomfortable.-- I was so ill that I believe some alarm was excited. Luckily my own consciousness & prescence of mind were quite sufficiently aline to enable me to assure the bystanders they had nothing to apprehend. I was fearfully cold (& this tho' I had taken every precaution as to clothing). After being laid down, quantities of blankets & feather pillows heaped all round & upon me, heat applied to my hands & feet, & good rubbing at the same time, I very soon came quite to. & tho I certainly suffered most acutely for the time, yet it appears to have

left no effects behind it.-- But some of the symptoms were very peculiar, & show the curious tendency of my constitutions, which seems never to alter in its ultimate nature, tho' circumstances & increased strength may leave the peculiarities for months & years together undevelopped.

Well-- on the whole all has been most prosperous. I think here is a fine gleam so I will put this into the post, & run off after the Hen. I dare say she may write you a line herself [a few words obscured by a fold in the paper] her letters are not gone. Post leaves rather early.
